title: ranunculus arvensis f. tuberculatus
intro: Ranunculus arvensis f. tuberculatus, also known as the corn buttercup, is a flowering plant species in the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and hairy leaves. The plant is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ranunculus arvensis f. tuberculatus include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in the form of infusions, tinctures, and creams, which are often used to treat various skin conditions and promote overall well-being.
